subject:
Re: MCore Error due to: System.IO.DirectoryNotFoundException: Could
 not find a part of the path '...m/refinement_temp/c_elegans/worker0'.
body:
So I think I have found the culprit. I think M is doing something strange 
to the star files. When I give it a .star file (coordinates binned by 2, so 
max X/Y dimensions = approx. 2000) that looks like this: 

=========================================================
# version 30001

data_optics

loop_ 
_rlnOpticsGroup #1 
_rlnOpticsGroupName #2 
_rlnSphericalAberration #3 
_rlnVoltage #4 
_rlnImagePixelSize #5 
_rlnImageSize #6 
_rlnImageDimensionality #7 
           1 opticsGroup1     2.700000   300.000000     4.780000           
96            3 
 

# version 30001

data_particles

loop_ 
_rlnCoordinateX #1 
_rlnCoordinateY #2 
_rlnCoordinateZ #3 
...
...
368.060000  1368.060000   744.060000    -28.93335    71.432991   113.227920 
TS_0071.tomostar     8.400000 
../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0071/TS_0071_0000000_4.78A.mrc 
../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0071/TS_0071_0000000_ctf_4.78A.mrc   
  4.780000            1            1     -4.22610     5.333901     5.333901 
           1     1.000000            1 2.984956e+06     0.234658           
10 
 1071.432000    85.001000   404.060000   174.982458    68.345719   
 -10.55141 TS_0065.tomostar     7.200000 
../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000000_4.78A.mrc 
../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000000_ctf_4.78A.mrc   
  4.780000            1            1     0.553901     -0.84425     0.553901 
           1     1.000000            1 2.978322e+06     0.131800           
 8 
  932.688000  1938.060000   444.688000    63.409574    43.800054   
 34.390241 TS_0065.tomostar     7.200000 
../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000001_4.78A.mrc 
../warp_tiltseries/group_1/subtomo/TS_0065/TS_0065_0000001_ctf_4.78A.mrc   
  4.780000            1            1     -0.84425     -0.84425     5.333901 
           1     1.000000            2 2.980775e+06     0.073959           
14 
=========================================================

and use the following create species command (rescaling to bin1 which is 
2.39 Å):

MTools create_species \
--population m/c_elegans.population \
--name ribosome_initial \
--diameter 360 \
--temporal_samples 1 \
--half1 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half1_class001_unfil.mrc \
--half2 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half2_class001_unfil.mrc \
--mask relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job040/mask.mrc \
--particles_relion relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_data.star \
--angpix_resample 2.39 

then the [Species_Name]_particle.star in 
m/species/[species_name]/[species_name]_particles.star has totally wrong 
coordinates which look like they've been multplied a lot:

=========================================================

data_

loop_
_wrpCoordinateX1 #1
_wrpCoordinateY1 #2
_wrpCoordinateZ1 #3
_wrpAngleRot1 #4
_wrpAngleTilt1 #5
_wrpAnglePsi1 #6
_wrpRandomSubset #7
_wrpSourceName #8
_wrpSourceHash #9
  1763.55298  6533.99316  3551.27295   -28.93335   71.43299   113.22792  1 
 TS_0071.tomostar  ZTx8rfiNPvMw0Xze-kry6nP9KYY
  5120.89160   407.14905  1930.85291   174.98245   68.34572   -10.55141  1 
 TS_0065.tomostar  NOBDUlh2lIXbCXxbftrH6l2w5UQ
  4459.09326  9264.77246  2120.27466    63.40957   43.80005    34.39024  2 
 TS_0065.tomostar  NOBDUlh2lIXbCXxbftrH6l2w5UQ

=========================================================

Will do some more digging and will post a reply when I find a fix.
Thanks
Euan



On Friday, 22 November 2024 at 12:31:32 UTC+1 Euan William Pyle wrote:

> Awesome, thanks, that has solved the bug! 
>
> One issue that is persisting however is that after running MCore as pasted 
> below, my 11 Å structure becomes a 34 Å one, and the resulting denoised 
> species looks like junk. The filtered species looks more like a ribosome, 
> but very noisy, and the features that I used to see outside the map (i.e. 
> neighbouring ribosomes) have disappeared which is suggesting to me that 
> perhaps M is not reading the particles in the correct place and is fitting 
> noise. I've tried with both the run_data.star and a relion_star_downgrade-d 
> (by Dynamo2M) star when creating species (see below) and correctly 
> specified angpix_coords, again for certainty.  I've also pasted the 
> original .xml files back into warp_tiltseries etc., and re-extracted 
> particles at bin1, and run relion_reconstruct, and my ribosome appears 
> looking very nice, so I'm a bit at a loss as to what's happening.
>
> Thanks again,
> Euan
>
>
>
> MCore \
> --population m/c_elegans.population \
> --refine_imagewarp 4x4 \
> --refine_particles \
> --perdevice_refine 4
>
>
> MTools create_species \
> --population m/c_elegans.population \
> --name job032_ribosome \
> --diameter 360 \
> --temporal_samples 1 \
> --half1 relion_ribosomes/Reconstruct/job032_half1_class001_unfil.mrc \
> --half2 relion_ribosomes/Reconstruct/job032_half2_class001_unfil.mrc \
> --mask relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job038/mask.mrc \
> --particles_relion ./job032_particles.star \
> --lowpass 17 \
> --angpix_resample 2.39 \
> --angpix_coords 4.78 \
> --angpix_shifts 4.78
> On Thursday, 21 November 2024 at 23:58:41 UTC+1 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>
>> 2.0.0dev30 available once this finishes... 
>> https://github.com/warpem/warp/actions/runs/11963052857
>>
>> On Thu, 21 Nov 2024 at 14:50, Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>
>>> Hi Euan,
>>>
>>> Thanks for the kind words and the report, I found the bug and will push 
>>> up a fix now - the only thing that is supposed to happen after the line 
>>> where your refinement crashed is saving the results. Given that your 
>>> refinement has run without actually refining anything (--iter 0) you can 
>>> safely move on to actual refinements without worrying :-)
>>>
>>> I'd appreciate if you could report back whether you see the same issue 
>>> with normal refinements...
>>>
>>> Cheers,
>>>
>>> Alister
>>>
>>>
>>>
>>> On Thu, 21 Nov 2024 at 14:33, Euan William Pyle <euan...@embl.de> wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hey gang, 
>>>> first thing - WARPTools is awesome and helping me a lot.
>>>>
>>>> I'm trying out M now, and have come across some strange behaviour.
>>>>
>>>> On a previous dataset, it worked absolutely fine, no issues.
>>>>
>>>> However, on my current dataset I'm having issues running: 
>>>>
>>>> MCore \
>>>> --population m/c_elegans.population \
>>>> --iter 0
>>>>
>>>> See the commands and the error message below.
>>>>
>>>> A few things that might be noteworthy:
>>>>
>>>> 1. I'm using dev version 29.
>>>> 2. I'm extracting subtomos in 3D and refining in rln4 (this worked fine 
>>>> on other dataset)
>>>> 3. I have 5 data sources which are arranged in directories like so: 
>>>>     ./warp_tiltseries/group_1/
>>>>     ./warp_tiltseries/group_2/
>>>>     ./warp_tiltseries/group_3/
>>>>     etc.
>>>>
>>>> As per the error, the m/refinement_temp/c_elegans is already empty.
>>>>
>>>> Strangely too, the resolution of the map was 10 A or so prior to M
>>>> and M reports a resolution of 38.24 here after refining. 
>>>>
>>>> Any ideas on what's going on? I feel a bit stooopid as I can't quite 
>>>> get what's going wrong.
>>>>
>>>> Thanks!
>>>> Euan
>>>>
>>>> P.S. I tried to revert back to the pre-M state by copying the original 
>>>> .xml files
>>>> from versions and placing them back in the warp_tiltseries directory
>>>> and then deleting the .source files and the M directory. Hopefully this 
>>>> didn't mess things up.
>>>> I re-extracted particles and ran relion_reconstruct as a sanity check 
>>>> and they look fine 
>>>>
>>>> ====================================
>>>> COMMANDS
>>>>
>>>> MTools create_population \
>>>> --directory m \
>>>> --name c_elegans
>>>>
>>>> MTools create_source \
>>>> --name c_elegans \
>>>> --population m/c_elegans.population \
>>>> --processing_settings group_5_tiltseries.settings
>>>>
>>>> #Repeat for groups 1 to 4.
>>>>
>>>> MTools create_species \
>>>> --population m/c_elegans.population \
>>>> --name ribosome \
>>>> --diameter 360 \
>>>> --temporal_samples 1 \
>>>> --half1 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half1_class001_unfil.mrc \
>>>> --half2 relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_half2_class001_unfil.mrc \
>>>> --mask relion_ribosomes/MaskCreate/job039/mask.mrc \
>>>> --particles_relion relion_ribosomes/Refine3D/job020/run_data.star \
>>>> --lowpass 11 \
>>>> --angpix_resample 2.39
>>>>
>>>> MCore \
>>>> --population m/c_elegans.population \
>>>> --iter 0
>>>>
>>>> ====================================
>>>> ERROR
>>>> ...
>>>> ...
>>>> ...
>>>> Commiting changes in c_elegans...Done
>>>> Saving intermediate refinement results for c_elegans...Done
>>>> Preparing population for data source c_elegans...Done
>>>> Loading gain reference for c_elegans... Done
>>>> Refining all series in data source...
>>>> 2/2                                                                     
>>>>       
>>>> Commiting changes in c_elegans...Done
>>>> Saving intermediate refinement results for c_elegans...Done
>>>> Finishing refinement
>>>> Gathering intermediate results, then reconstructing and filtering...
>>>> ribosome: 38.24 �  
>>>>
>>>> 1/1Unhandled exception. System.Exception: Something went wrong during 
>>>> refinement. Sorry! Here are the details:
>>>>
>>>> System.IO.DirectoryNotFoundException: Could not find a part of the path 
>>>> '/g/mattei/euan/Krios/20240926_C_Elegans_LO/m/refinement_temp/c_elegans/worker0'.
>>>>    at System.IO.FileSystem.RemoveEmptyDirectory(String fullPath, 
>>>> Boolean topLevel, Boolean throwWhenNotEmpty)
>>>>    at System.IO.Directory.Delete(String path, Boolean recursive)
>>>>    at MCore.MCore.DoProcessing()
>>>>    at MCore.MCore.DoProcessing()
>>>>    at MCore.MCore.Main(String[] args)
>>>>    at MCore.MCore.<Main>(String[] args)
>>>> run.sh: line 44: 762754 Aborted                 (core dumped) MCore 
>>>> --population m/c_elegans.population --iter 0
>>>>
>>>> ====================================
>>>>
>>>> -- 
>>>> You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>>>> Groups "Warp" group.
>>>> To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
>>>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>>>> To view this discussion visit 
>>>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/1b382000-43f6-46e7-86e9-a269e03f0858n%40googlegroups.com 
>>>> <https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/1b382000-43f6-46e7-86e9-a269e03f0858n%40googlegroups.com?utm_medium=email&utm_source=footer>
>>>> .
>>>>
>>>
